message:
fix(net): introduce AcceptWaiters for fair multi-accept wakeup on TCP and Unix listeners

Previously, TcpListener and VirtualTcpListener stored a single Waker
for accept notifications. When multiple tasks concurrently called
poll_accept, only the last registrant would be woken on connection
arrival — earlier waiters would starve indefinitely.

This introduces AcceptWaiters, a shared waker registry that:
- Tracks all pending accept waiters (deduped via will_wake)
- Implements Wake so it can be registered with the reactor as a
  single composite waker
- Provides wake_all() for close/error paths and wake_others(current)
  for successful accepts (the current acceptor doesn't need re-waking)

TcpListener changes:
- register_interest() no longer takes &Context; it constructs a Waker
  from Arc<AcceptWaiters> and registers that with the reactor
- poll_accept registers the caller's waker with AcceptWaiters, then
  calls wake_others on success and error paths
- schedule_accept_retry takes &Arc<AcceptWaiters> instead of &Context
- Test: listener_fanout_wakes_all_pending_accept_waiters

VirtualTcpListener changes:
- Replaced single-waker Option<Waker> with AcceptWaiters
- inject_connection and close now call wake_all
- VirtualConnectionInjector carries AcceptWaiters clone

UnixListener: parallel changes matching TcpListener's AcceptWaiters
pattern.

TCP split (split.rs): replaced .unwrap() with .expect() for combined
waker access.

TCP traits (traits.rs): renamed serve() to serve_sequential() with a
warning doc section explaining it handles connections sequentially.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
